---
title: "About Me"
---

I am **Dharanish Kedarisetti**, based in Boston. Applied ML engineer with 5+ years building production models and edge systems — currently a Research Software Engineer at Amazon working on smart cart technology.

Interests: Machine Learning, Climbing, Meditation, and [Photography](https://500px.com/p/dharanish_90).

## Experience

**Amazon** — Research Software Engineer II, Boston, MA *(Oct 2022–present)*
Smart cart technology (Dash Carts). Rust software for 1k–5k edge devices on AWS, shopping receipt diagnosis, CI/CD pipelines, Qualcomm NNP integration, and custom camera driver development.

**Motorola Solutions** — Sr. Machine Learning Engineer, Somerville, MA *(Jan 2020–Sept 2022)*
Smart camera features for context similarity, unusual activity detection, and crowd analysis. CNN model deployment via ONNX, Docker, and Kubernetes. Contributed to the patent committee.

**Avigilon** — Research Engineer / Software Engineer, Somerville, MA *(Feb 2018–Jan 2020)*
Video analytics framework development focused on unusual activity detection in C++ and Python.

## Education

**Northeastern University**, Boston, MA — M.Sc., Telecommunication Systems Management, 2017

**BITS Pilani**, India — M.S., Physics (Honors) & B.E., Electronics & Instrumentation (Honors), 2013

## Patents

- [US11303877B2](https://patents.google.com/patent/US11303877B2/en) — **Method and System for Enhancing Use of Two-Dimensional Video Analytics by Using Depth Data** (Avigilon Corp, granted 2022). Improves 2D video analytics by fusing depth data to validate object classifications, correcting for shadows, reflections, and distance-based size distortions. With Pietro Russo, Peter L. Venetianer, Mahesh Saptharishi.

- [US20210350117A1](https://patents.google.com/patent/US20210350117A1/en) — **Anomalous Pose Detection Method and System** (Motorola Solutions, 2021). Uses neural networks to generate skeletal pose vectors and flags deviations from learned normal-behavior clusters in real time. With Teresa Dankel, Patrick Henson, Aral Sarrafi.

- [WO2020210504A1](https://patents.google.com/patent/WO2020210504A1/) / [US11302117B2](https://patents.google.com/patent/US11302117B2/en) — **Anomaly Detection Method, System and Computer Readable Medium** (Avigilon Corp / Motorola Solutions, granted 2022). Detects unusual behavior in surveillance video by modeling statistical baselines from object metadata (class, location, velocity) per scene cell — without processing raw frames. With Peter Venetianer.

- [WO2020051704A1](https://patents.google.com/patent/WO2020051704A1/) — **System and Method for Improving Speed of Similarity Based Searches** (Avigilon Corp, 2020). Accelerates appearance-search queries by k-medoid clustering of selected feature vectors before database search, reducing compute without sacrificing result quality. With Nicholas John Alcock, Peter L. Venetianer.

- [US12634398](https://patents.google.com/patent/US12634398/en) — **Method of Generating a Trigger Initiating an Emergency Incident Response Workflow** (Motorola Solutions, 2026). Receives emergency notification data and retrieves associated sensor data by time and location to automatically initiate incident response. With Emmy Beltre, Kaveh Malakuti, Mariya Bondareva.

## Papers

- **Large-scale Indoor Mapping with Failure Detection and Recovery in SLAM** — Sharmin Rahman, Rob DiPietro, Dharanish Kedarisetti, Vinod Kulathumani. *IROS 2024*. Proposes an automated visual-inertial SLAM service with a health tracker for faulty measurements and continuous session merging, validated in grocery stores spanning 1700–3700 m². [[PDF]](https://cdn.amazon.science/4d/c7/f15d4d9a4c1d976df4e975144d3e/large-scale-indoor-mapping-with-failure-detection-and-recovery-in-slam.pdf) [[Amazon Science]](https://www.amazon.science/publications/large-scale-indoor-mapping-with-failure-detection-and-recovery-in-slam)

- **Plus Disease in Retinopathy of Prematurity: Improving Diagnosis by Ranking Disease Severity and Using Quantitative Image Analysis** — Jayashree Kalpathy-Cramer, J Peter Campbell, Deniz Erdogmus, Peng Tian, Dharanish Kedarisetti, et al. *Ophthalmology* 123(11):2345–2351, 2016. Shows that while experts disagree on categorical ROP diagnosis, pairwise severity ranking yields good inter-expert correlation (mean r = 0.84), and that computer-based image analysis can model this continuous spectrum. [[DOI]](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ophtha.2016.07.020) [[PubMed]](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/27566853/)

## Links

[GitHub](https://github.com/kedarisetti)
